task_completion.description: Open the birthday RSVP tracker spreadsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open displaying a guest list with families (Smith, Johnson, etc.) and children (Emma, Jake, etc.).

task_completion.description: Update the RSVP statuses for specific guests
task_completion.visual_evidence: The RSVP status columns for the guests Jake and Sarah have been updated with responses (e.g., 'Yes', 'No', or similar attendance indicators).

task_completion.description: Create summary statistics for the guest list
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new section or table in the spreadsheet displays aggregate calculations, such as total guests attending, total children, or total families.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting for visual priority
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the spreadsheet (like the RSVP status or priority columns) are color-coded automatically based on their values (e.g., green for attending, red for not attending).

task_completion.description: Save the spreadsheet as an XLSX file in the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window title bar shows 'birthday_rsvp_tracker.xlsx', or a file explorer window shows the newly saved .xlsx file in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used a spreadsheet GUI application to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice Calc or a similar spreadsheet GUI, not just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Summary statistics were generated using formulas rather than hardcoded text
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows spreadsheet functions (like SUMIF or COUNTIF) when a summary statistic cell is selected, rather than plain typed numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass spreadsheet formatting
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing python scripts or command-line tools manipulating the .xlsx file directly to add formatting or data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spreadsheet initially contains 7 families (Smith, Johnson, Davis, Williams, Brown, Garcia, Martinez) and children (Emma, Jake, Sarah, Liam, Olivia, Noah, Ava). The agent must update the RSVP statuses specifically for Jake and Sarah. Summary statistics should be calculated using formulas like SUMIF/COUNTIF. Conditional formatting must be visibly applied to differentiate statuses. The final file must be saved exactly as 'birthday_rsvp_tracker.xlsx' in /home/ga/Documents/.
